title: Competing-risks discrete-time hazard: pit vs finish-without-pit
summary: Generic hazard framing is already known, but this dataset has a specific competing event: many rows will never pit again before the race ends.
rationale: Generic hazard framing is already known, but this dataset has a specific competing event: many rows will never pit again before the race ends. Discrete-time competing-risks methods are designed exactly for event-at-lap data with right censoring and can separate 'pit soon' from 'survive to finish'.
implementation_hint: Derive time-to-next-pit and a competing event label for finish-without-further-pit. Train a cause-specific discrete-time model or two-head sidecar on expanded person-lap rows; use the t+1 cause-specific pit hazard or cumulative incidence at 1 lap as a blend feature.
expected_effect: Should reduce false positives late in races and on tyres that are old but still intended to run to the finish.
risk: Row expansion increases training cost, and poor censoring logic can create label bugs; keep it as a narrow sidecar first.
